channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Powerful US storms bring threats ranging from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma, damaged a high school near Dallas, and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dan Goldman looks to be 'the man in the arena' against Trump
item.description: Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) wasn’t planning to run for Congress after his stint as the lead counsel on President Trump’s first impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation into Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted subs on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: How Democrats will respond to Trump's Congress address
item.description: The administration says the theme will be the renewal of the American dream and comes amid sweeping cuts to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: NASA's two stuck astronauts are finally closing in on their return to Earth after 9 months in space
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts are just a few weeks away from finally returning to Earth after nine months in space.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles County probation officers charged with staging 'gladiator fights' at juvenile hall
item.description: More than two dozen Los Angeles County probation officers are charged with child abuse for so-called "gladiator fights" at a juvenile detention facility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man sentenced to 4 years in prison for Culver City hate crime assault
item.description: A previously homeless man has been sentenced to more than four years in prison for an unprovoked racially motivated assault on an Asian American woman in Culver City. Jesse Allen Lindsey, 38, pleaded guilty in December to a federal hate crime charge for the 2021 attack. Around 1 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Trump's speech to Congress will offer divided nation an accounting of turbulent first weeks
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Restaurants owners who support tipped wage bill were doxxed: Dems
item.description: "We want to make it clear that these actions are unacceptable," Speaker Julie McCluskie and House Majority Leader Monica Duran said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Texas school district apologizes for sending student data to wrong families
item.description: AISD confirmed that a link including students' data is now inactive, and any families who received the data cannot access it.
item.dc:creator: Brynne Herzfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Johnson retires from Fox Sports after being face of NFL coverage for most of 3 decades
item.description: Jimmy Johnson, the Pro Football Hall of Fame coach who won two Super Bowls and a college national championship, announced his retirement from Fox Sports on Monday after being part of its NFL coverage for most of the network's 31 years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AirPods dying? Professor discovers microenvironment that may be responsible
item.description: UT-Austin associate professor Yijin Lu said a similar microenvironment exists in all sorts of electronics. This includes places like your phone and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Retired US military leaders support letting Israel 'finish the job' against Iran, proxies
item.description: Dozens of retired U.S. military leaders urge President Donald Trump to throw America's support behind Israel in its fight against a nuclear Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State forgives more than $7M in medical debt through new initiative
item.description: Medical debt forgiven through the initiative is considered a no-strings-attached gift that does not count as earned income or incur taxes.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: More endangered Mexican gray wolves are roaming the southwestern US, annual survey shows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — It is a ritual that takes place every winter — federal and state wildlife managers use remote cameras, scat collection, radio telemetry devices and helicopters to count Mexican gray wolves that are roaming mountain ranges in parts of New Mexico and Arizona.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Despite weather concerns, New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras Day and bids farewell to Carnival season
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The good times and parades still rolled in New Orleans for Mardi Gras Day celebrations early Tuesday, ahead of expected severe winds, thunderstorms and tornado warnings in the afternoon.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump claims American campus 'agitators' will be permanently expelled for 'illegal' protests
item.description: President Donald Trump claimed that "agitators" who partake in "illegal protests" on U.S. college campuses will be permanently expelled or even "imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans target 4 'sanctuary' cities as Trump pushes mass deportations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress are taking aim at four cities — often called “sanctuary cities” — over their policies limiting cooperation with immigration enforcement with a hearing this week that comes as President Donald Trump presses ahead with his campaign of mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 9 states report measles cases as outbreaks grow
item.description: One of the most contagious viruses in the world is spreading around the United States. So far in 2025 there have been three distinct measles outbreaks and at least 164 cases reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention coming from nine states.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge chooses not to sanction lawyer who admitted using AI in mistake-filled brief
item.description: U.S. District Judge Thomas Cullen will not sanction attorney Thomas Guyer for submitting a brief filled with incorrect citations and misquotes made by AI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Google makes it easier to remove your personal info
item.description: Google is making removing your personal information from search results much easier. Now, when you search for yourself and see a link that contains information like your email, address or phone number, you can request a takedown in seconds.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Tributes pour in for R&B singer Angie Stone after her death at 63 in a highway crash
item.description: Fans are remembering the voice and songs of trailblazer Angie Stone after her death at 63 in a weekend crash on an Alabama highway as she traveled from a performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP pushes ahead with citizenship voting bill. Some state election officials say it's problematic
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The centerpiece election legislation from congressional Republicans would require voters to prove their citizenship when registering, raising concerns among state election officials about how it would be implemented and who would pay for it.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Best mullet? Best use of Taylor Swift? AP hands out its own movie awards ahead of the Oscars
item.description: Hollywood's never-ending awards season can make it easy to get a little lost in the run-of-the-mill “best” categories. Actor. Actress. Director. Picture. A great movie can be reduced to a single performance; those that are left out seem to simply vanish for a while.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4
